Airbus raises stakes in engine dispute with Pratt & Whitney: Reuters

Airbus's damages claim against Pratt & Whitney escalates supply chain accountability for engine delivery failures, signaling manufacturers will pursue legal remedies when supplier disruptions threaten production schedules and financial performance.

Boeing 777-9 Hits Massive FAA Milestone: Is The Long Wait Finally Over?

With this green light from the FAA, Boeing is one step closer to its goal of certifying its long-awaited 777-9 aircraft.

Boeing's 777-9 achievement of a major FAA milestone accelerates the certification timeline for this wide-body aircraft, potentially reshaping long-range commercial aviation capacity and Boeing's competitive position against Airbus.

AeroVironment's $200 million ESAero acquisition strategically consolidates electric UAS manufacturing and design capabilities to accelerate production and product portfolio expansion in the growing unmanned systems market.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

How Long Did American Airlines Operate The Boeing 747 For?

Find out how long American Airlines flew the Boeing 747 and what led to the early retirement of the iconic “Queen of the Skies.”

American Airlines' early retirement of the Boeing 747 reflects the broader industry shift toward fuel-efficient twin-engine widebodies, a transition that reshaped long-haul fleet economics for major carriers.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class

Ten explorers are currently training at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to become flight-eligible astronauts. Selected in 2025, the astronaut candidates are building the technical and operational skills needed for future missions to the International Space Station, the Moon, and eventually...

NASA's 2025 astronaut class represents the pipeline of human spaceflight talent that will support commercial space station operations and lunar/deep space exploration missions, creating opportunities for aerospace contractors and launch providers.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically develop and implement controls while understanding that residual risk acceptance is a critical decision point that must be documented and justified under regulatory requirements.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests

NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope team recently blasted the observatory with extreme sound, shook it, and listened to its electronic hum. Roman passed all three assessments, which aimed to confirm that the observatory will withstand launch conditions and function as expected in space. The ...

NASA's Roman Space Telescope successfully completed rigorous environmental qualification testing, demonstrating that advanced space observatories require the same systematic validation protocols that aerospace manufacturers apply to aircraft and launch vehicles before operational deployment.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

How One Of The World's Most Notorious Fraudsters Posed As A Pilot To Fly For Free

Made extra-famous through the hollywood film ‘Catch Me If You Can’, legendary conman turned security consultant Frank Abagnale managed to fly for free aboard some 250 flights by posing as a deadheading pilot - a flight crew member being repositioned to another city for operational purposes. In a ...

Frank Abagnale's historical exploitation of crew positioning procedures highlights critical vulnerabilities in airline identity verification systems that modern aviation security must continuously address to prevent unauthorized flight access.

See Simple Flying →

Continued Russian airspace incursions into NATO member states demonstrate persistent regional tensions and validate the critical importance of robust air defense coordination and rapid NATO response capabilities in the Eastern European theater.

The DJI Mini 4K's aggressive pricing strategy continues to democratize drone technology accessibility, potentially expanding the consumer market segment that aviation authorities must increasingly monitor for airspace compliance and safety oversight.

NASA's proposed acceleration in robotic lunar landing cadence signals intensified space infrastructure development that could create new opportunities for aerospace contractors and commercial space partnerships supporting sustained lunar operations.

The growing market for small GEO satellites demonstrates how specialized space infrastructure companies are enabling new business models in satellite communications, creating opportunities for aerospace supply chain expansion.

Everdrone raises SEK 36 million – enters commercial phase for drone services in emergency healthcare

Everdrone currently operates the world’s first operational drone service integrated into the emergency response chain

uAvionix's ping978EC transceiver addresses the UK's evolving regulatory requirements for beyond-visual-line-of-sight drone operations, signaling a market shift toward compliance-driven avionics solutions for UAS operators.

US drone ban has a growing exemption list

The United States has exempted four foreign-made drones from its sweeping import ban. See the latest additions to a list that’s quietly getting longer… more…

The US drone import ban's expanding exemption list signals regulatory flexibility that could reshape competitive dynamics between domestic and foreign UAS manufacturers while creating uncertainty for operators seeking compliant equipment.

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers a critical pathway for airlines and airports to simultaneously expand capacity and reduce environmental impact as global air traffic continues to surge.

See MDPI →

U.S. Air Force to Update U-2 Dragon Lady Defensive System

The iconic 70-year-old spy plane is receiving updates to its AN/ALQ-221 Advanced Defensive System. Despite ever changing plans to retire the type in the next few years, the U.S. Air Force’s U-2 Dragon Lady continues to play a crucial role in providing vital ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Re...

The U.S. Air Force's continued investment in modernizing the U-2's defensive systems demonstrates the platform's enduring operational value despite recurring retirement plans, highlighting the critical importance of ISR capabilities in contemporary defense strategy.

The iconic 70-year-old spy plane is receiving updates to its AN/ALQ-221 Advanced Defensive System. Despite ever changing plans to retire the type in the next few years, the U.S. Air Force’s U-2 Dragon Lady continues to play a crucial role in providing vital ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Re...

The U.S. Air Force's continued investment in modernizing the U-2's defensive systems demonstrates the platform's enduring operational value despite recurring retirement plans, highlighting the critical importance of ISR capabilities in contemporary defense strategy.

See The Aviationist →
